title: Rhammatophyllum flexuosum
intro: Rhammatophyllum flexuosum, also known as the "flexuous rhammatophyllum," is a type of medicinal plant belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Rhammatophyllum flexuosum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, such as rhammatin and flexuosin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional medicine and is prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory and inflammatory conditions.

Rhammatophyllum flexuosum is a species within the genus Rhammatophyllum, which is a monotypic genus containing only this species.
